decomposition_difference: Nuclear ss Whirl: IFPA counts ss as +0; FM treats ss as ADD-additive
wording_template: FM decomposes this as nuclear (+2) + ss (treated as +1) + whirl (3) for 6 then subtracts; IFPA's canonical math treats same-side (ss) as +0 (Red 2026-05-11) for nuclear (+2) + whirl (3) + ss (+0) = 5.
notes: Pure SS pattern; pedagogically rich because the positional-operator distinction is structural

decomposition_difference: Compression: blurry = stepping paradox (pt11 transitive); both readings reach 5
wording_template: In IFPA's framing both readings work: the compressed form 'blurry whirl' (where blurry contributes +1 through the stepping-paradox structure) and the expanded form 'stepping (+1) + paradox (+1) + whirl (3)' arrive at the same 5 ADD. The shorter folk name is what players say; the structural reading is no less correct.
notes: Pt12 transitive-blurry packet resolved into this; pedagogically illustrates stopping-depth ambiguity

decomposition_difference: Compression: blurry transitively expands the compound torque base
wording_template: Same compression template as Blurry Whirl with torque substitution
notes: Demonstrates that the transitive-blurry rule applies to compound bases not only primitives

decomposition_difference: Compression: blurry blender ≡ stepping paradox blender ≡ stepping paradox whirling op osis
wording_template: The trick's name is a folk compression of stepping paradox blender. Both readings reach 6: blurry (+1) + blender (4) = 5? No — blurry transitively expands so the count is 1 + 1 + blender (4) = 6 only when blurry contributes through stepping-paradox alongside an existing whirling. The Red ruling clarified this is structurally 'stepping paradox blender'.
notes: The most pedagogically rich case because it cascades compression through a compound base

decomposition_difference: Two valid stopping depths: gyro torque = 5; spinning ss miraging osis = 5
wording_template: Two valid decompositions reach the same ADD: 'gyro torque' (gyro +1 + torque 4 = 5) and the deep form 'spinning ss miraging osis' (spinning +1 + ss +0 + miraging +1 + osis 3 = 5). Players say mobius; analysts can unfold to either depth. The language picks its own stopping point.
notes: The dictionary's first explicit multi-description equivalence showcase

decomposition_difference: Hidden X-dex carries +1 through 'atomic mirage' compound
wording_template: The straight count would be atomic (+1 non-rotational on mirage) + mirage (2) = 3 — but atom smasher carries an implicit X-dex from a toe that adds +1, giving 4. This 'hidden +1' is what makes atomic-on-rotational-base = +2 rather than +1 in some contexts; the X-dex is the mechanical reason.
notes: Wave 2 Q3 will refine the X-dex preservation rule; this row is safe to use as an example BECAUSE the Red ruling explicitly named atom-smasher

decomposition_difference: Barraging contributes through dex-multiplication: 2 dexes + osis = 5
wording_template: Baroque (= Barraging Osis) decomposes as two dexes plus the osis base — barraging's contribution is structural (it multiplies the dex count) rather than modifier-additive. Two dexes (+2) + osis (3) = 5.
notes: Wave 2 Q6 will determine whether barraging is a body modifier or set primitive generally; the Baroque ruling is named and safe to cite

decomposition_difference: Recursive operator: FM names 'Atomic Eggbeater' but eggbeater already IS atomic legover (pt4)
wording_template: FM's 'Atomic Eggbeater' applies atomic twice — once via the canonical eggbeater (which is atomic legover per pt4) and again as the surface modifier. IFPA's canonical math doesn't apply atomic recursively: atomic (+1) + legover (3) = 4 already covers the structural meaning.

decomposition_difference: FM-over discrepancy: FM 'Furious Whirl' = 7 by FM's rotational escalation; IFPA pt6 + pt10 settled furious +2 rotational + whirl 3 = 5
wording_template: Genesis (FM-named 'Furious Whirl' at 7 ADD) is +2 above IFPA's canonical 5. The +2 traces to FM's earlier rotational-escalation convention which IFPA retired in pt10 (rotational bases get a flat +1 per modifier rather than progressive scaling).
notes: Edge case; illustrates historical-drift pattern without controversy
